primary_complaint: Bilateral knee pain, right greater than left, x 3+ years
functional_limitations: Cannot use stairs without hand rail
Pain limits ability to demonstrate drills as volunteer coach
objective_measurements: Right knee flexion limited to 95 degrees

imaging_findings.key_findings: X-ray (02/15/2025): Right knee: Severe tricompartmental osteoarthritis with bone-on-bone medial compartment involvement, Kellgren-Lawrence Grade IV, varus deformity 8 degrees. Left knee: Moderate OA, KL Grade III, less severe than right.

surgical_approach_if_mentioned: Anterior approach. Will perform standard primary TKA.

denial_risk_flags.label: Cortisone Injection Dates Undocumented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The two corticosteroid injections are dated only as 'approx 2023, exact date unclear,' which does not meet payer documentation standards requiring specific dates for each injection administered. Aetna PPO typically requires precise dates to verify the timeline of conservative care failure.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Obtain and document the exact dates of the cortisone injections from the administering provider's records or pharmacy records, and add a chart addendum specifying those dates.

denial_risk_flags.label: Physical Therapy Duration at Minimum Threshold
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Physical therapy is documented at exactly 6 weeks, which is the minimum threshold many payers require; some Aetna PPO policies require 6–8 weeks of documented PT with specific session counts and measurable outcomes before approving CPT 27447.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a chart addendum specifying the number of PT sessions attended, the treating facility (Lakeside Rehab is noted), measurable functional outcomes at discharge, and confirmation that the full course was completed without improvement.
denial_risk_flags.anchorText: PT at Lakeside Rehab for 6 weeks

denial_risk_flags.label: No MRI Documented for Surgical Authorization
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Only X-ray imaging is documented. While KL Grade IV on X-ray is strongly supportive, some Aetna PPO policies for CPT 27447 require or prefer MRI to rule out other pathology and confirm surgical necessity, particularly in patients under 70.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Confirm whether Aetna PPO's current clinical policy for CPT 27447 requires MRI; if so, order and document MRI results prior to submission, or include a clinical justification for why X-ray alone is sufficient.

denial_risk_flags.label: Hyaluronic Acid Injection Duration Not Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The Synvisc-One injection dated 06/2024 has no documented follow-up period or duration of observation before declaring failure, which payers may flag as insufficient time to assess treatment response.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a chart addendum documenting the follow-up assessment date after the 06/2024 Synvisc-One injection and the specific findings that confirmed treatment failure at that visit.
denial_risk_flags.anchorText: Synvisc-One hyaluronic acid injection right knee 06/2024

denial_risk_flags.label: Elevated BMI May Require Pre-Op Weight Loss
denial_risk_flags.explanation: BMI of 34.2 is documented. Some Aetna PPO policies for total knee arthroplasty require documentation that BMI-related surgical risk has been addressed or that weight loss was attempted, particularly when BMI approaches or exceeds 35.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a note documenting that the surgical risk associated with BMI 34.2 was discussed with the patient and that the attending surgeon has determined the patient is an appropriate surgical candidate despite this finding.

diagnosis_codes.note: ICD-10 codes M17.11 and M17.12 are present, specific, and laterality-coded appropriately for the requested procedure.

conservative_treatments_named.note: Four distinct conservative treatments are named: physical therapy, corticosteroid injections, hyaluronic acid injection, and OTC analgesics/NSAIDs.

conservative_treatment_duration.note: Physical therapy has a documented duration (6 weeks), but corticosteroid injections and Synvisc-One injection lack documented duration of observation, and OTC medications have only 'ongoing' without a specific timeframe.

imaging_findings.note: X-ray with KL Grade IV, bone-on-bone medial compartment, and varus deformity is documented with a date, providing strong radiographic support for surgical necessity.

functional_limitations.note: Three specific functional limitations are documented: walking limited to 1 block, stair use requiring handrail, and inability to perform coaching duties.

surgical_approach.note: Anterior approach is explicitly documented along with confirmation of standard primary TKA.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 27447 is explicitly documented and matches the requested procedure of right total knee arthroplasty.

symptom_duration.note: Symptom duration of 3+ years is explicitly documented in the HPI.
